publicationStmt.p: Transcribed from Bodleian Library, Lovelace Byron collection, Box 165, folios 190-191. Transcription by Lisa Gold (2022). TEI conversion for Sextant pipeline, 2026.

div.p: I have posted your letter to the Hen. You have blundered about her stay in Town, which I wonder at, as I sent you her letters to me about it.
I did not forget about the Medallion. Wyon is the man to take my likeness; but he has

of late declined all private commissions. Wheatstone thinks however that when he has been introduced to me, & knows that I am the lady in question, he will not object. Wheatstone only mentioned a lady. There is no one, Wheatstone says, at all to compare with him for medallion likenesses.-- Then the subsequent -typing is quite a separate
affair. But really I don't think I am pretty enough to be medallioned at all.
So Emily is in Town; She left her Card here yesterday. I enclose it.
Wedding-cake has arrived from Mr & Mrs Kay Shuttleworth.
The Miss Neville who is to be your Aunt John is no relation whatever of the Neville Grenvilles.
